steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how the poem is structured overall. Lines 1–2 present impressions others have of the speaker (‘Let them think I love them more,’ ‘Let them think I care’). Lines 3–4 dismiss those impressions (‘what is it to me / Who am self-complete’).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The speaker illustrates the importance of self-confidence and then explains how it helped her in several cases.: the lines are not imagined scenarios but impressions others hold. ✗
  • Choice B — The speaker is sad about the lack of understanding among people around her and then expresses a desire to connect with them.: the speaker shows no sadness; the tone is dismissive indifference. ✗
  • Choice C — The speaker presents herself as fiercely independent and then recounts scenarios that demonstrate that independence.: the speaker explicitly does not care about others' opinions, the opposite of a desire to connect. ✗
  • Choice D — The speaker lists various impressions people might have of her and then explains why they don't matter to her.: exactly maps onto lines 1–2 (impressions) and lines 3–4 (self-sufficiency that renders them irrelevant). ✓
That is the correct answer.
